# Luis Dourado

> Personal website for Luis Dourado, a senior software engineer writing about backend systems, developer tools, Git workflows, agent-assisted engineering, local infrastructure, and product-minded software delivery.

This site contains selected work, essays, and notes. Prefer the linked text endpoints when available because they are generated for terminal and language-model reading with reduced layout noise.

## Core Pages

- [Home](https://www.luisdourado.com/terminal.txt): Overview of Luis Dourado, featured projects, latest writing, and important links.
- [About](https://www.luisdourado.com/about.txt): Background, engineering focus areas, and contact links.
- [Work](https://www.luisdourado.com/work.txt): Selected projects with descriptions, status, stack, and repository links.
- [Writing](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog.txt): Blog index with post summaries, dates, tags, and links.
- [RSS](https://www.luisdourado.com/rss.xml): Feed for published writing.

## Featured Writing

- [The Development Server Beside The Router](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog/development-server-beside-the-router.txt): Moving development work from a MacBook to a permanent Linux server accessed through Remote SSH and Tailscale.
- [Direction Is Not Memory](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog/direction-is-not-memory.txt): Why repeated agent corrections in real repositories need scoped repository direction instead of more transcript memory.
- [What An Agent Harness Actually Owns](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog/what-an-harness-owns.txt): Runtime boundaries around agentic tools, provider adapters, tool execution, extensions, and local trust.
- [The Branch Nobody Could Explain](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog/the-branch-nobody-could-explain.txt): How large-repo branch chaos turned into a local-first workflow for declaring dependencies and building integration branches.
- [The Private Layer Beside A Meeting](https://www.luisdourado.com/blog/meeting-fog.txt): A private note layer, transcript-aware chat, and always-on-top assistant for meeting-heavy work.

## Projects

- [Fabric](https://github.com/lutefd/fabric): Git-backed protocol for scoped repository direction, lifecycle records, projections, receipts, and causal provenance across agent work.
- [Luc](https://github.com/lutefd/luc): Terminal AI agent with a streaming TUI, local tool execution, OpenAI-compatible providers, and runtime extensions.
- [Weaver](https://github.com/lutefd/weaver): Local-first Go CLI for stacked Git branches, dependency DAGs, stack sync, and ephemeral integration branches.
- [Atlas](https://github.com/lutefd/atlas): Local-first incident evidence workspace for immutable raw evidence, derived timelines, search, exports, and OCR.
- [Lay](https://github.com/lutefd/lay): Always-on-top Wails and Svelte meeting assistant for notes, AI chat, hotkeys, opacity, and gateway routing.

## Optional

- [GitHub](https://github.com/lutefd): Source repositories and public development work.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisfadourado/): Professional profile.
- [Email](mailto:lfaddourado@gmail.com): Contact.
